work_package.title: Operationalize Temporary UX/UI Standards as Non-Authoritative Responsive Guidance
work_package.current_state: A temporary UX/UI standards policy was explored in closed unmerged PR #185, but repository truth has no approved catalog-backed contract defining how such standards may inform Responsive decisions without becoming authority or correctness evidence.
work_package.target_state: A bounded, schema-backed guidance contract classifies approved UX/UI standards, applicability, exceptions, and evidence limits while preserving repository authority, responsive decision ownership, and all readiness boundaries.

measurable_current_state.statement: No approved catalog-backed contract currently governs temporary UX/UI standards guidance; PR #185 is closed and unmerged.
measurable_current_state.measurement: Measured by absence of an authoritative local guidance contract, schema, fixtures, validator, and STATUS/CI parity on main.

measurable_target_state.statement: Temporary UX/UI standards are represented as non-authoritative guidance with explicit applicability, exception, provenance, and false-claim boundaries.
measurable_target_state.measurement: Accepted only when contract/schema, positive and negative fixtures, deterministic diagnostics, docs/STATUS parity, and exact-head CI pass without changing Responsive, Kernel, Project Gate, evidence, or readiness authority.

target_capability.statement: Responsive can consume a bounded standards guidance artifact as decision support while rejecting authority substitution, unsupported universality, stale provenance, and correctness upgrades.
target_capability.measurement: A schema-backed contract, deterministic validator, negative coverage, CI wiring, command-index, and STATUS parity remain mutually consistent.

work_package.expected_project_delta: Common UX/UI standards become reusable, reviewable decision-support inputs rather than implicit prompt prose, while repository contracts and real responsive validation remain authoritative.
allowed_work: Define a non-authoritative UX/UI standards guidance contract with provenance, applicability, exceptions, and scope.
Add valid and negative fixtures for authority substitution, unsupported universal rules, stale provenance, conflicting standards, and forbidden correctness upgrades.
Add deterministic validation and align CI, command-index, documentation, and STATUS where directly affected.
forbidden_work: Do not make temporary UX/UI guidance a replacement for repository contracts, Stage Anchors, validators, Kernel decisions, Project Gate authority, or project-specific evidence.
Do not create submitted evidence, mutate Issue #8, or run or authorize real pilot execution.
Do not claim production_ready, release_ready, live_render_validated, export_json_validated, accessibility_passed, pixel_perfect, or responsive_correctness_validated.
Do not treat standards conformance, CI success, merged PRs, catalog completion, or Work Package completion as domain evidence.
must_deliver: Non-authoritative UX/UI standards guidance contract and schema.
Deterministic validator with positive and negative coverage for provenance, applicability, conflicts, authority substitution, and boundary upgrades.
Primary validation-chain, command-index, documentation, and STATUS parity where directly affected.
must_preserve: Rolling queue remains a historical reconciled archive, not the execution driver.
Controller selection must come only from planning/EV4_AUTOMATION_WORK_PACKAGE_CATALOG.json.
Quality gates, CI, review, and evidence boundaries remain mandatory.
Technical evidence claims remain false unless real evidence gates pass.
Catalog replenishment is state-driven and must not block active Work Package execution.
Catalog replenishment must respect the single-active-mutation-PR policy.
definition_of_done: The measurable target state is met by schema-backed or validator-backed repository evidence.
The selected PR slice remains reviewable and stays under this Work Package ID.
No repository authority, submitted evidence, pilot authorization, or forbidden readiness/correctness claim is produced by the Work Package.

split_rule.principle: A Work Package may be large in objective, but each PR must remain reviewable.

allowed_pr_slices.title: standards guidance contract and schema
allowed_pr_slices.objective: Define provenance, applicability, exception, conflict, and non-authority semantics.
allowed_pr_slices.review_boundary: Reviewable PR slice under the same Work Package ID; no unrelated micro-objective may be introduced.

allowed_pr_slices.title: guidance fixtures, validator, and CI
allowed_pr_slices.objective: Add deterministic positive and negative coverage plus primary validation-chain wiring.
allowed_pr_slices.review_boundary: Reviewable PR slice under the same Work Package ID; no unrelated micro-objective may be introduced.

allowed_pr_slices.title: guidance documentation and STATUS parity
allowed_pr_slices.objective: Record allowed use, exceptions, provenance requirements, and remaining evidence/readiness limitations.
allowed_pr_slices.review_boundary: Reviewable PR slice under the same Work Package ID; no unrelated micro-objective may be introduced.

blocked_if: The guidance would replace or bypass repository contracts, validators, Stage Anchors, Kernel or Project Gate authority.
Source provenance, applicability scope, or conflict resolution cannot be represented without asserting unsupported universal rules.
Repository checks or standards conformance would be promoted to responsive-correctness or readiness evidence.
completion_record_requirements: Record selected Work Package ID and PR slice ID in the PR body.
List files changed, validators changed, and CI commands run.
State commands not run and why.
Record whether catalog replenishment was checked and whether it was non-mutating, same-PR in-scope, or blocked by single-active-PR policy.
Record remaining risks and the next safe human action.
